title: Little Big Mom

jokes.text: D'oh! [Homer crashes into something]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer presumably driving or skiing
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation as he crashes
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase is perfectly integrated to his character and economical, though not original by Season 11

jokes.text: I like the ones where the mouse kills the cat
jokes.setup: Discussion about Itchy and Scratchy show
jokes.punchline: Bart's straightforward preference for cartoon violence
jokes.explanation: Simple character-appropriate line showing Bart's love of cartoon violence

jokes.text: Well, anyone would be nervous with all the economic turmoil you read about in the-
jokes.setup: Marge trying to deflect from hiding something in a box
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at distraction with a completely irrelevant topic
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge deflection technique, well-structured misdirection

jokes.text: Get her! She's doing something!
jokes.setup: Marge acting suspiciously nervous
jokes.punchline: The immediate assumption that any suspicious behavior warrants attack
jokes.explanation: Economical line showing the family's paranoid reaction to Marge's spring cleaning

jokes.text: It's the Goodwill truck! She cleaned out the attic!
jokes.setup: The family's panic about what Marge is hiding
jokes.punchline: The 'threat' is just charitable donation
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations - the big secret is just spring cleaning

jokes.text: Our junk! And the exits are sealed! Go to plan 'B'!
jokes.setup: Discovering Marge is donating their stuff
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a military operation with backup plans
jokes.explanation: Great escalation turning spring cleaning into an action movie scenario

jokes.text: We came this close to losing our spare Christmas tree stand. You monster!
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically saving items from donation
jokes.punchline: The 'precious' item is completely mundane and useless
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Homer's attachment to useless objects with dramatic language

jokes.text: Look! Here's the box for my Pitch Back. If I still had it, I could put it in here!
jokes.setup: Homer defending keeping junk
jokes.punchline: He's keeping the box for something he doesn't even have anymore
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that's perfectly Homer - keeping packaging for something he lost, with circular reasoning

jokes.text: This flash cube has two flashes left
jokes.setup: Defending keeping old junk
jokes.punchline: Treating a nearly dead flash cube as valuable
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's inability to throw anything away

jokes.text: You bought them after the Nagano Olympics and never skied once
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out their unused ski equipment
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to how long they've been unused
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about impulse purchases

jokes.text: Hey, let's go skiing right now!
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing their impulsive purchases
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proves her point with another impulsive decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Homer's complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Look, Dad. It's the drummer from Bread
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out someone on the ski lift
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reference to an obscure 70s soft rock band member
jokes.explanation: Funny for its specificity and randomness - typical Simpsons deep-cut reference

jokes.text: See, Mom? You conquered your fears and now you're ready to- / I'm sorry!
jokes.setup: Lisa encourages Marge about overcoming fear
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately panics and presumably pushes Lisa off
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but fairly predictable physical comedy

jokes.text: The Widowmaker. No, that one's for the ladies. Spinebuster? Boring. Whoo! Colostomizer!
jokes.setup: Homer choosing increasingly dangerous ski slopes
jokes.punchline: The absurdly medical/surgical name for a ski slope
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with creative wordplay for ski slope names, 'Colostomizer' is particularly inventive

jokes.text: Hi-diddly-ho, schuss-in-boots!
jokes.setup: Ned greeting someone on the slopes
jokes.punchline: His characteristic word modification combining skiing terms
jokes.explanation: Typical Ned wordplay that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, it allows for maximum mobility. Feels like I'm wearing nothin' at all
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his tight ski suit
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently sexual description that makes everyone uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the 'Stupid sexy Flanders' sequence, perfectly innocent Ned being accidentally provocative

jokes.text: Must wash eyes! Stupid sexy Flanders!
jokes.setup: Ned's repeated 'nothin' at all' line echoing in Homer's head
jokes.punchline: Homer's disturbed reaction and iconic phrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became a meme. Perfect escalation and character work showing Homer's disturbed reaction

jokes.text: This is the worst pain ever!
jokes.setup: Homer breaking his leg skiing
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction to injury
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer overreaction, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Single [chuckles]
jokes.setup: Reporting on Homer's condition
jokes.punchline: Casual, cheerful delivery of what sounds like bad news
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in the casual delivery suggesting Homer might be alone/single now

jokes.text: Leave those deer alone! But they were trying to eat me
jokes.setup: Lisa apparently being chased by deer
jokes.punchline: The idea that peaceful deer would attack a person
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual gag playing with expectations about harmless woodland animals

jokes.text: Can I go to the bathroom? Uh-uh! Say it in snowboard. Um, I gotta blast a dookie. Dook on!
jokes.setup: Instructor insisting on snowboard lingo for everything
jokes.punchline: Crude bathroom terminology in fake snowboard speak
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that works for the snowboard culture satire context

jokes.text: Is this seat taken? Uh, I think that's an armrest
jokes.setup: Disco Stu trying to sit next to Marge
jokes.punchline: He's so eager he mistakes furniture for a seat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Disco Stu's desperation and poor awareness

jokes.text: Sure, baby. Whatever your trip is. Disco Stu wants you to be comfortable while he does his thing
jokes.setup: Marge's innocent party interpretation
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu's overly accommodating but still creepy response
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Disco Stu's desperate adaptability

jokes.text: Skiing fanny first into a crevasse isn't my idea of fun
jokes.setup: Explaining why she stayed inside
jokes.punchline: Specific and undignified description of her skiing accident
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy description of Marge's skiing mishap

jokes.text: Oh, man. Another clock accident
jokes.setup: Someone getting injured at the lodge
jokes.punchline: Treating clock accidents as routine
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist touch suggesting clock accidents are common at this lodge

jokes.text: I want my wife to get the best treatment money can buy. Whoa, whoa, whoa. Don't go nuts. Uh, better than that. Perfect
jokes.setup: Homer wanting the best care for Marge
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracking when considering the cost
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's conflicted priorities between love and money

jokes.text: Otherwise, a hunk of bone could zoom right to her brain [chuckles]
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Marge's injury
jokes.punchline: Casual, cheerful delivery of horrifying medical information
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor in the doctor's inappropriate cheerfulness about serious injury

jokes.text: I can't be in the hospital. Tomorrow is laundry day. And I've gotta de-meat Lisa's bologna
jokes.setup: Marge worrying about being hospitalized
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific domestic task about removing meat from bologna
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Marge moment - the specific task of 'de-meating' processed meat is perfectly weird

jokes.text: All the laundry and dirty dishes will be there when you get back
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reassure Marge
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admitting he won't do any housework
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - trying to be reassuring but revealing his laziness

jokes.text: Just make sure your father eats all his meals over a tarp. But not the good tarp. I want you to get married on that someday
jokes.setup: Marge giving Lisa household advice
jokes.punchline: Treating a tarp as a future wedding venue
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing both the family's poverty and Marge's practical optimism about repurposing household items

jokes.text: You are wasting thousands of dollars worth of interferon. And you're 'interferon' with our good time [laughs]
jokes.setup: Discussion about expensive medicine
jokes.punchline: Forced pun on medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Weak forced pun that feels like lazy writing - the setup is too obvious

jokes.text: Dad, where are your clothes? I don't know. Don't tell me Mom dresses you. I guess. Or one of her friends
jokes.setup: Homer appearing undressed
jokes.punchline: Revealing his complete dependence on others for basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Homer's infantile dependence on Marge

jokes.text: Come on, bikini inspector. Scrub toilet? Okay, that was a practice
jokes.setup: Drawing chores from a hat
jokes.punchline: Bart hoping for an impossible 'chore' then dismissing real work as practice
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Bart's avoidance of responsibility and juvenile fantasies

jokes.text: Lisa, I'll do the dishes when I pick it out of the chore hat and it's not a practice
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer to do dishes
jokes.punchline: Homer creating elaborate rules to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - using the system against itself to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: Oh! But Lenny just got some bottle rockets. You stay away from Lenny
jokes.setup: Lisa telling Bart not to trade food for firecrackers
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately mentioning another dangerous trade opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Bart's focus on dangerous toys and Lisa's maternal concern

jokes.text: That's not true. He left it on the bus. You're dead, squealer!
jokes.setup: Bart lying about his sweater
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately exposing the lie and Bart's violent threat
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling dynamic humor - Lisa's honesty vs Bart's deception

jokes.text: Oh, I don't want to be a bother. Wouldn't bother us. It just turns up your morphine
jokes.setup: Marge not wanting to use call button
jokes.punchline: Revealing the button just drugs her more
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about hospital indifference and over-medication

jokes.text: The cheese isn't quite melted. Let me just pop it back in the- [growls] Here you go
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to perfect dinner preparation
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressive hunger preventing proper cooking
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Homer's impatience with Lisa's perfectionism

jokes.text: Slivered almonds for the green beans. I'll be right back.
jokes.setup: Lisa serving dinner to Homer and Bart
jokes.punchline: Her perfectionist attention to detail while they devour food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's high standards amid chaos

jokes.text: Bart, you were supposed to scour the pans. They need to soak. You said that four days ago. Look! They're rusted through. It's an illusion
jokes.setup: Lisa confronting Bart about dirty pans
jokes.punchline: Bart's ridiculous denial of obvious rust damage
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character work showing Bart's stubborn refusal to admit fault

jokes.text: Astronaut bread? It's the bread of astronauts
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Homer's grocery choices
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular, confident explanation of a nonsensical product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - tautological explanation delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Maple soda? A cell phone full of candy? Astronaut bread? It's the bread of astronauts.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Homer's bizarre grocery choices
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical justification that astronaut bread is 'the bread of astronauts'
jokes.explanation: Great absurd product names and Homer's circular logic defense

jokes.text: I didn't know Aerosmith made a cereal. Dad, I gave you a list. Oh, yeah [laughs] You were way off
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Homer's shopping
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming Lisa's list despite ignoring it completely
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's complete inability to take responsibility

jokes.text: Hello? Lollipop Island? There's a little girl here who had too many sour balls
jokes.setup: Lisa getting frustrated with household management
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish response to her stress
jokes.explanation: Good Homer infantilism - treating Lisa's legitimate stress as a childhood tantrum

jokes.text: Marco. Polo. Marc- Ohh! [splash]
jokes.setup: Playing Marco Polo in what's presumably the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Someone falling into the toilet during the game
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy showing the house falling into chaos

jokes.text: Lucy McGillicuddy Ricardo Carmichael. And I think there's some more
jokes.setup: Lisa asking who Lucy is
jokes.punchline: Lucy's impossibly long married name from the show
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about Lucy's complex name history from the TV show

jokes.text: You mean like when you hid inside the conga drum to scare Ricky? Hey! Stay away from the drums! That's my bit
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting a Lucy-style scheme
jokes.punchline: Lucy being protective of her signature gags
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about TV characters being possessive of their famous bits

jokes.text: My skin! My beautiful skin!
jokes.setup: Bart discovering what he thinks are leprosy sores
jokes.punchline: His vain concern about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's unexpected vanity about his appearance

jokes.text: Welcome to Virtual Doctor. From the makers of Dragon Quest and Sim Sandwich
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart consulting a medical computer
jokes.punchline: Medical software from video game companies
jokes.explanation: Good satire of both video games and questionable medical technology

jokes.text: Duh! We're pigs!
jokes.setup: Computer asking about exposure to unsanitary conditions
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact admission of their filthiness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment of rare Homer self-awareness about their hygiene

jokes.text: Leprosy! I can't believe it! That fortune cookie was right!
jokes.setup: Reacting to leprosy diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Referring to an unmentioned fortune cookie prediction
jokes.explanation: Funny random reference suggesting Homer got a very specific and dark fortune

jokes.text: Why would God punish a kid? I mean, an American kid?
jokes.setup: Bart questioning divine justice
jokes.punchline: His assumption that American children should be exempt
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical line about American exceptionalism and childhood innocence

jokes.text: At Sunday school, they said the lepers were cured by some bearded dude. Jesus? Yeah, that sounds right
jokes.setup: Discussing potential cures for leprosy
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual description of Jesus and Homer's vague recognition
jokes.explanation: Good irreverent humor about their casual approach to religious figures

jokes.text: But I know someone who's even holier than Jesus
jokes.setup: Looking for someone to cure their leprosy
jokes.punchline: Suggesting someone is holier than Jesus (obviously Flanders)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for going to Flanders, with mild blasphemous humor

jokes.text: You saw Ben-Hur without me? We were broken up then. Well, that's no-
jokes.setup: Discussing lepers in Ben-Hur
jokes.punchline: Revealing marital separation over movie viewing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing the Flanders' relationship issues over trivial matters

jokes.text: Brains. Brains. Use your brains to help us. Your delicious brains
jokes.setup: Acting like zombies/lepers
jokes.punchline: Homer's hunger making him focus on the 'delicious' aspect
jokes.explanation: Good character touch showing Homer's appetite even while playing zombie

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no, no! I'm just thinking we could send you to a better place
jokes.setup: Homer accusing Ned of shunning them
jokes.punchline: Ned's ominous suggestion about sending them away
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that sounds like he's suggesting death before revealing Hawaii

jokes.text: Code blue! A thousand c.c.'s of leg wax! Stat!
jokes.setup: Marge's hairy leg revelation
jokes.punchline: Treating cosmetic grooming as a medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation treating beauty maintenance as life-or-death medical situation

jokes.text: He told us he'd been going to the gym. [chuckles] Wow
jokes.setup: Moe asking about Homer's absence from the bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's incredulous reaction to Homer claiming to go to the gym
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing everyone's disbelief at Homer exercising

jokes.text: Because I couldn't take that. I- I just couldn't. I- [sobbing]
jokes.setup: Moe worried Homer might be at another bar
jokes.punchline: His emotional breakdown over losing a customer
jokes.explanation: Great Moe character moment showing his desperate dependence on his few customers

jokes.text: Hansen's disease. You know, like that horrible cream soda
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining leprosy's proper name
jokes.punchline: Confusing Hansen's disease with Hansen's soda
jokes.explanation: Clever confusion between medical terminology and brand names

jokes.text: Cost us a bundle too. I guess we'll have an imagination Christmas this year. Yea! Imagination Christmas!
jokes.setup: Ned explaining the cost of shipping Homer and Bart to Hawaii
jokes.punchline: The kids being excited about an imaginary Christmas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing the Flanders family's ability to find joy in poverty

jokes.text: I got a pogo stick. I got a hula hoop
jokes.setup: Boys playing imagination Christmas
jokes.punchline: Pretending to receive classic toys
jokes.explanation: Sweet follow-up showing the innocent joy of the Flanders children

jokes.text: We'll take the cure, bag a few lobsters, then watch some gay guys get married
jokes.setup: Homer planning their Hawaii trip
jokes.punchline: Casual inclusion of gay marriage as tourist activity
jokes.explanation: Progressive humor for 2000, treating gay marriage as normal tourist attraction

jokes.text: Oh, you'll have a great view of the whole beach from the electric needle room. Really? The whole beach? Yes. From the electric needle room
jokes.setup: Homer asking about accommodations
jokes.punchline: Ominous emphasis on 'electric needle room'
jokes.explanation: Good ominous buildup with repetition emphasizing the scary treatment

jokes.text: I figured it out after I ate one of my chest sores
jokes.setup: Explaining how he knew it wasn't real leprosy
jokes.punchline: Disgusting method of discovery that's perfectly Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - disgusting but logical solution to the mystery

jokes.text: All we have to do is endure two hours of blinding pain. Then it's nothing but shopping and surfing
jokes.setup: Explaining why they want to stay in Hawaii
jokes.punchline: Treating torture as a minor inconvenience for vacation
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - accepting torture as reasonable price for vacation benefits

jokes.text: Tonight we're gonna put our fake sores back on, then jump the fence at Club Med and scare the normals
jokes.setup: Planning their Hawaiian activities
jokes.punchline: Using fake leprosy to terrorize tourists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart scheme - using their fake disease for pranks on 'normals'

jokes.text: Aloha- [electricity crackles] Aloha- Ow! Until we meet a- [screams]
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sing during electric needle treatment
jokes.punchline: Painful interruptions to his Hawaiian song
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Homer's determination to sing through pain

tmdb_overview: When Marge leaves the house to recuperate from a skating injury, everything goes to hell - until Lisa convinces Homer and Bart they have leprosy.
